news.title: Adani Properties highest bidder for MHADA’s Worli, Bandra redevelopment projects
news.para: Adani Properties has emerged as the highest bidder for MHADA’s cluster redevelopment projects at Adarsh Nagar in Worli and Bandra Reclamation, while a consortium comprising Hanura Realty, Chandak Realtors, Premsagar Infra Realty and Vantier Realty has emerged as the highest bidder for the S.V.P. Nagar project in Andheri West.

news.title: Welspun One plans to lease over 10 million sq ft in next three years
news.para: Welspun One plans to lease over 10 million sq ft of warehousing and industrial real estate in the next three years, nearly doubling its current operational footprint. The company is expanding into adjacent segments like in-city distribution and retail, with key projects including a large FTWZ development at Nhava Sheva and logistics parks in Pune and Bengaluru.

news.title: NGT sets up panel to probe ‘illegal brick kiln, clay mining in Balasore
news.para: The National Green Tribunal has ordered a joint investigation into allegations of illegal brick kiln operations and unauthorized brick clay mining in Balasore district. A multi-agency panel will verify the charges and recommend corrective measures. The tribunal directed the panel to submit its report within one month. Notices have been issued to state officials and private parties.

news.title: ED arrests Earth Infrastructures' promoters in ₹2,004-crore buyer fraud case involving over 19,000 buyers
news.para: Four promoters of Earth Infrastructures Ltd. were arrested by the Enforcement Directorate in a Rs 2,004-crore homebuyer fraud probe. Over 19,000 buyers and investors were allegedly defrauded as projects remained incomplete. The ED claims Rs 467 crore was siphoned off, with proceeds used to acquire assets. Investigations stem from Delhi Police FIRs and an SFIO complaint.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh acquires 100 hectares in Ghaziabad for industrial hub
news.para: Ghaziabad administration has acquired approximately 100 hectares of land in Modinagar's Niwari for a new industrial hub. Farmers received compensation at four times the circle rate, marking a significant step in boosting industrial infrastructure and attracting investment in the NCR region. This development addresses the long-standing demand for land for new industries in Ghaziabad.

news.title: How ULCRA held back Kolkata's growth and why its repeal could be a turning point for Bengal
news.para: Kolkata's urban growth faced decades of constraint due to the Urban Land (Ceiling and Regulation) Act, 1976. This law hindered large-scale development and investment. Now, the state government is considering its repeal. This move promises to unlock opportunities for real estate and infrastructure. It could simplify land acquisition and boost economic growth across West Bengal.

news.title: Chandigarh civic body nets record ₹61 crore property tax in rebate window
news.para: Chandigarh's municipal corporation achieved a record Rs 61 crore in property tax during the April-May rebate period, surpassing last year's collection. Commercial properties contributed significantly, with residential units also showing strong participation. Taxpayers who missed the deadline now face a 25% penalty and 12% interest.

news.title: Uttarakhand HC extends stay on demolition of private properties for road widening in Haldwani
news.para: Uttarakhand High Court has extended its interim stay on demolishing private properties in Haldwani, halting a road-widening project. Petitioners, including hotel owners, claim demolition notices were issued with insufficient notice, violating procedures. They argue authorities are evicting them under the guise of removing encroachments without a fair hearing. The next hearing is scheduled for June 4.

news.title: Lucknow development body reopens bookings for premium flats
news.para: The Lucknow Development Authority (LDA) has reopened registrations for 394 flats across its Aishbagh Square and Park View Apartment projects.  These premium residential units, featuring three-bedroom configurations with servant rooms or study options, will be allotted through a lottery system.  Construction is slated for completion within three years, with possession expected within five.

news.title: Over 35,000 rehabilitation colony residents may get ownership rights in Chandigarh
news.para: Chandigarh administration is set to grant ownership rights to over 35,000 residents in rehabilitation colonies. The proposed policy, sent to the Home Ministry, initially targets original allottees and their legal heirs occupying units for over seven years. Future phases may include GPA holders, who constitute a significant portion of current occupants.

news.title: Don’t rush to raze illegal buildings: West Bengal CM
news.para: Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari has cautioned the Kolkata Municipal Corporation's buildings department against hasty demolitions without prior notice. He emphasized a careful approach, especially for buildings occupied by economically backward sections, urging adherence to legal procedures and opportunities for regularization of minor violations.

news.title: Chandigarh administration to auction 10 residential sites from June 2
news.para: Chandigarh administration is set to e-auction 10 residential plots ranging from 100 to 502 square yards later this month. Applications and earnest money deposits are open from June 2 to June 23, with reserve prices between Rs 3.3 crore and Rs 16.5 crore. The auction process will conclude by June 29.

news.title: SC pulls up Jaipur development body over delay in action on commercial use of residential areas
news.para: The Supreme Court has severely criticized the Jaipur Development Authority (JDA) for its non-compliance with court directives regarding commercial establishments in residential areas. The JDA failed to appear or submit an affidavit detailing actions taken after surveys, despite ongoing resident complaints. The court emphasized the planning authority's accountability for enforcing town planning norms and protecting residential zones.

news.title: HC limits relief in building violation cases, clears way for DTCP action in Gurugram
news.para: Authorities can now act on numerous building violation cases in Gurgaon after the Punjab and Haryana High Court narrowed a previous restraint order. The court clarified the order applies only to parties involved in litigation, allowing action against others with alleged unauthorized constructions and commercial activities in residential areas. This paves the way for enforcement proceedings.

news.title: Ghaziabad: Consumer forum orders builder to hand over original deed & compensate homebuyer
news.para: A Ghaziabad consumer commission has ordered developers of Shipra Krishna Srishti to hand over the original sale deed of a flat to HDFC within 15 days. The builders failed to provide the document as required by a tripartite agreement, leading to a compensation order of Rs 50,000 for the flat buyer.

news.title: Punjab RERA orders builder to refund charges, secure completion certificates
news.para: Rera has ordered a builder to refund Rs 5.45 lakh in excess maintenance charges and charges for 342 sq ft of excess area to allottees. The authority also mandated the builder to obtain Completion and Occupation Certificates and pay 12% annual interest on sale consideration until compliance.

news.title: Hyderabad: Second land parcel in Raidurg sold at ₹204 crore per acre
news.para: Hyderabad's prime real estate is fetching record prices, with TGIIC securing Rs 204 crore per acre for a Raidurg plot, sold to Vamsiram builders for Rs 1,040 crore. This follows another auction where a similar plot fetched Rs 237 crore per acre. These sales highlight developer confidence and Hyderabad's growth as a business hub.

news.title: Noida: Societies' upkeep bills set to go up with labour code’s higher wages mandate
news.para: New labor codes mandating higher minimum wages are significantly increasing monthly maintenance bills for residents in NCR condominiums. Manpower costs, forming the bulk of budgets, are projected to rise by 20-30%, forcing many societies to revise Common Area Maintenance (CAM) charges upwards.

news.title: ED arrests real estate businessman Vikram Wadhwa in ₹645-crore IDFC First Bank 'fraud' case
news.para: The ED has arrested real estate businessman Vikram Wadhwa, the third arrest in a money laundering probe involving Rs 645 crore embezzled from Haryana government, Chandigarh administration, and two schools. Wadhwa allegedly received over Rs 70 crore in crime proceeds and played a key role in layering and concealing laundered funds.

news.title: Unica Capital buys Monaco property asset for $31.4 million
news.para: European firm Unica Capital has acquired Le Montaigne, a Monaco residential property, for €27 million. This deal underscores the strength of the 'ultra prime' property market despite global economic challenges. Unica Capital's CEO Byron Baciocchi stated that Monaco remains a key focus for the company. The Le Montaigne residence will be available for rent at €180,000 per month.
